headline: East Malaysia Special Grant Frozen at RM26.7 Million
context: The special grant to Sabah under Article 112C has remained at RM26.7 million since 1969 despite constitutional requirements for periodic review.

cards.big: Sabah special grant frozen at RM26.7 million since 1969
cards.sub: Inflation-adjusted value now less than 5% of original purchasing power

cards.big: If adjusted for inflation grant should be approximately RM850 million in 2026
cards.sub: Federal government ignored 5 formal review requests from Sabah since 2000

cards.big: Article 112C mandates review after every census but none conducted
cards.sub: Constitutional lawyers say non-review amounts to a federal breach of constitution

cards.big: Sarawak equivalent grant mechanism replaced by SST arrangement in 2023
cards.sub: Sabah demands similar treatment or immediate adjustment of frozen amount

cards.big: A frozen grant since 1969 tests whether constitutional obligations are enforceable
RM26.7 million special grant — frozen since 1969 — would be RM800 million inflation-adjusted today.
